free_text: Evidence for the deuteron-nucleus tensor interaction
The deuterons are identified by using
E-dE telescopes, mounted on two rotatable arms.
A monitoring detector was fixed at 45. degr. so as
to furnish a relative renormalization for the
Downstream of the main scattering chamber was placed a
carbon polarimeter which furnished a continuous
monitoring of the beam polarization. This polarimeter
was in fact a scattering chamber of 40 cm diameter,
containing a (CH2)n target and two solid-state detector
placed at 45 degr. left and right of the beam direction
The beam particles were collected by a Faraday cup
The positioning accuracy of the telescopes
was about +- 0.1 degr. and their angular aperture was
